steps.title: Test the exclusivity claim
steps.content: The students claim broken-wing display is exclusive to shorebirds, the order Charadriiformes. To weaken that claim, the table must show birds outside Charadriiformes that perform the display.

steps.title: Reject shorebird-only evidence
steps.content: A is consistent with the claim because it pairs a displaying shorebird with a non-displaying non-shorebird. B and C show variation among shorebirds but do not show the behavior outside shorebirds. Sooty grouse and pine warbler are non-shorebirds that perform the display. The answer is D.
